cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking why weekend afternoons would become dead time without sports programs. The options are A through D. First, I need to look back at the passage provided. The passage mentions that sports are often held on Saturday and Sunday afternoons, which are described as the slowest time periods of the week for general television viewing. So without sports, these time slots would be considered "dead time." The passage says that sports events are the most popular weekend programs, especially among male viewers who may not watch much TV at other times. This suggests that without sports, there's less appeal for viewers during those afternoons, leading to lower viewership. Looking at the answer choices, option A says "Because there would be few viewers." That seems to align with the passage's point that without sports, these time periods are slow. The passage also mentions that TV networks can sell advertising time at high prices during these times because sports attract viewers. If there were no sports, the viewership would drop, making those time slots less valuable for advertisers, hence "dead time." Option B says advertisers would be off work. I don't recall the passage mentioning anything about advertisers' work schedules. The focus was on viewership and the attractiveness of sports to certain demographics. So B might not be correct. Option C says TV programs would go slowly. The passage doesn't mention the speed of the programs. "Dead time" here likely refers to low ratings, not the pacing of the shows. So C might not be right.
